article:
In brief: we're in for a hot and relatively dry week ahead in Abuja, with temperatures consistently reaching the mid-30s and only a slight chance of rain on a few days. If you're planning on spending time outdoors, make sure to stay hydrated and take breaks in the shade, especially during the peak sun hours around noon. Today, the temperature is already at 34°C, but with a feels-like temperature of 39°C due to the humidity, so it's essential to take care of yourself.

As we take a look at the current conditions, the humidity is at 52%, which is relatively moderate for this time of year. The wind is blowing at a gentle 4 km/h, providing some relief from the heat. However, with a UV index of 8, it's crucial to protect yourself from the sun's strong rays, especially if you plan on visiting the National Assembly Complex or taking a stroll along the shores of Jabi Lake. Don't forget to wear sunscreen, a hat, and sunglasses to safeguard your skin.

Looking ahead to the rest of the week, it's clear that we're in for a prolonged period of hot weather. The high pressure system dominating our region is not showing any signs of weakening, which means we can expect temperatures to remain elevated. On [MAP: a satellite image of the high-pressure system], you can see the extent of the high pressure covering the entire country, with Abuja right in the center. This high pressure is also responsible for the relatively low chance of rain throughout the week.

### 2026-04-07
For Tuesday, we're expecting more of the same, with a high of 34°C and a low of 26°C. There's a 40% chance of rain, but it's likely to be isolated and short-lived. If you're planning on attending a meeting at the Federal Secretariat, you might want to check the forecast one last time before heading out, just in case. However, it's unlikely that any rain showers would be intense or long-lasting enough to cause significant disruptions. On [MAP: a radar image of the rain showers], you can see the scattered nature of the precipitation, with most areas staying dry.

As we head into the evening, the temperature will gradually drop, but it will still be quite warm, especially in areas with dense vegetation like the National Park. If you're planning on taking a walk or going for a run, make sure to stay hydrated and wear light, breathable clothing. The humidity will remain relatively moderate, which should provide some relief from the heat.

### 2026-04-08
On Wednesday, we're expecting a slight decrease in temperature, with a high of 33°C and a low of 25°C. The chance of rain also decreases to 35%, making it even less likely that we'll see any significant precipitation. If you're planning on visiting the Abuja National Mosque, you can expect plenty of sunshine and warm weather, making it an excellent day to take in the beautiful architecture and surroundings. On [MAP: a visibility map of the city], you can see how clear the skies will be, with excellent visibility throughout the day.

As the day wears on, the heat will become more pronounced, especially in areas with little shade, such as the open spaces around the National Stadium. Make sure to take breaks and stay hydrated, especially if you're engaging in physical activity. The UV index will remain high, so don't forget to protect yourself from the sun's strong rays.

### 2026-04-09
Thursday is looking like it could be the most interesting day of the week, weather-wise. With a high of 35°C and a low of 25°C, it will be one of the hotter days, and there's also a 50% chance of rain. This increase in precipitation probability is due to a weak low-pressure system moving into the area, which could bring some isolated thunderstorms. If you're planning on attending an event at the International Conference Centre, you might want to check the forecast one last time before heading out, just in case. On [MAP: a map of the low-pressure system], you can see how it's moving into the region, bringing a slight chance of thunderstorms.

As the low-pressure system moves through, we can expect some increased cloud cover, which should provide some relief from the heat. However, it's unlikely that the rain will be widespread or long-lasting enough to cause significant disruptions. The evening will still be warm, with temperatures only gradually dropping as the night wears on.

### 2026-04-10
On Friday, we're expecting a return to the hot and dry weather that has characterized the rest of the week. With a high of 39°C and a low of 25°C, it will be one of the hottest days, and there's only a 3% chance of rain. If you're planning on spending time outdoors, make sure to stay hydrated and take breaks in the shade, especially during the peak sun hours around noon. On [MAP: a temperature map of the city], you can see just how hot it will be, with temperatures soaring across the entire region.

As the day wears on, the heat will become even more intense, especially in areas with dense urban development, such as the Central Business District. Make sure to take care of yourself and avoid heat stroke by staying cool and hydrated. The UV index will remain high, so don't forget to protect yourself from the sun's strong rays.

### 2026-04-11
The weekend is looking like it will be more of the same, with plenty of sunshine and warm weather. On Saturday, we're expecting a high of 36°C and a low of 26°C, with only a 5% chance of rain. If you're planning on visiting the Abuja National Zoo, you can expect plenty of warm weather, making it an excellent day to take in the sights and sounds of the zoo. On [MAP: a map of the zoo], you can see the various attractions and exhibits, and plan your day accordingly.

As the day wears on, the heat will become more pronounced, especially in areas with little shade, such as the open spaces around the zoo. Make sure to take breaks and stay hydrated, especially if you're engaging in physical activity. The evening will still be warm, with temperatures only gradually dropping as the night wears on.

### 2026-04-12
On Sunday, we're expecting more of the same, with a high of 37°C and a low of 26°C, and only a 3% chance of rain. If you're planning on taking a walk or going for a run, make sure to stay hydrated and wear light, breathable clothing. The humidity will remain relatively moderate, which should provide some relief from the heat. On [MAP: a map of the city's parks and green spaces], you can see the various areas where you can take a stroll or go for a jog.

As we head into the evening, the temperature will gradually drop, but it will still be quite warm, especially in areas with dense vegetation, such as the National Park. Make sure to take care of yourself and avoid heat stroke by staying cool and hydrated.

### 2026-04-13
Looking ahead to next Monday, we're expecting a high of 37°C and a low of 26°C, with an 18% chance of rain. While it's still unlikely that we'll see any significant precipitation, it's possible that we could see some isolated showers moving into the area. If you're planning on attending a meeting at the Federal Secretariat, you might want to check the forecast one last time before heading out, just in case. On [MAP: a forecast map of the region], you can see the slight chance of rain moving into the area, and plan your day accordingly.

As we head into the rest of the week, it's clear that we're in for a prolonged period of hot and dry weather. Make sure to stay hydrated, take breaks in the shade, and protect yourself from the sun's strong rays. We'll be keeping a close eye on the forecast and will let you know if anything changes. Stay cool, Abuja!
